ミュージアムの裏はすぐ富士見町駅、線路の向こうに南アの入笠山、村に来た熊はたぶんこの山から来てる、とても会いたくないわね…
この立体ジオラマで行く場所の地形を確認したかった、山に登るわけではない、原付で奥地を走る為です、そんな使い方をするのは私だけ？
山奥でなく駅前徒歩30秒の駐車場の崖です、放置された原付ナンバーの地名は今は無き増穂町でした、中々根性のあるスクーター最期の地

images.alt:
​Starters (Entrées)
​French Onion Soup – Slow-cooked caramelized onions in a rich beef broth, topped with a crust of melted Gruyère cheese and toasted sourdough.
​Homemade Duck Foie Gras – Smooth duck liver terrine served with a seasonal fruit chutney and toasted brioche.
​Leeks with Vinaigrette – Tender poached leeks served chilled with a mustard-shallot dressing and crushed hard-boiled eggs (mimosa style).

​Main Courses (Plats)
​Steak Frites – Grilled entrecôte (r ibeye) steak served with a traditional Béarnaise sauce and a side of crispy, hand-cut golden fries.
​Duck Confit – Slow-cooked duck leg with crispy skin, served with Sarladaise potatoes (sautéed in duck fat with garlic and parsley).
​Sole Meunière – Whole Dover sole pan-fried in brown butter with lemon and parsley, filleted tableside and served with steamed baby potatoes.
​Beef Bourguignon – Tender beef braised for hours in a red wine sauce with carrots, pearl onions, mushrooms, and smoke d bacon lardons.

​Desserts
​Crème Brûlée – Silky vanilla bean custard topped with a thin, brittle layer of caramelized sugar.
​Profiteroles – Light choux pastry puffs filled with vanilla ice cream and drizzled with warm, dark chocolate sauce.
​Tarte Tatin – Famous "upside-down" caramelized apple tart, served warm with a dollop of Isigny crème fraîche.
​Floating Island (Île Flottante) – Soft poached meringue peaks resting on a bed of vanilla crème anglaise, finished with caramel drizzle and toasted almonds

images.alt: The visual is a stark, high-contrast black and white portrait that radiates intimidating power and ethereal presence. The setting is a void-like, endless white space, isolating the subject completely. I am standing tall, wearing a simple, flowing floor-length dress that catches an invisible breeze, creating dramatic, sweeping folds around my legs. Despite the softness of the dress, my posture is rigid and unyielding. My hands are clasped loosely in front of me. My face is the focal point—dead center, filling the frame. My expression is a solid, unshakeable glare, eyes narrowed slightly, looking directly through the camera lens with an intensity that feels heavy. The lighting is flat and unforgiving, exposing every texture of skin and fabric without mercy. The vibe is commanding and transcendent—"I am here, I am vast, and I am not an object for your consumption."

images.alt: The visual is a gritty, photorealistic snapshot of a Montreal backyard evening, circa late 90s. The setting is a cramped, wooden deck attached to a red brick triplex. I am sitting in a mismatched plastic lawn chair, wearing an authentic, vintage Montreal Canadiens jersey—red, white, and blue—and slightly baggy jeans. I have a sweating bottle of beer in one hand, resting on my knee. The other hand is fiddling with a battered, silver transistor radio sitting on a small plastic table next to an overflowing ashtray. My expression is relaxed but distracted, looking off into the distance where the city lights are just starting to blur. The lighting is warm and golden, coming from a string of bare bulbs hanging above the deck, but the shadows are long and deep. The texture is grainy, like film shot at a high ISO. The vibe is intimate and melancholic—a moment of pause in the chaos of the city life.

images.alt: The visual is a mind-bending, hyper-realistic fusion of deep-sea mystery and the vastness of outer space. The setting is the cosmos, but instead of a vacuum, it's filled with bioluminescent, deep-sea darkness. I am floating weightlessly, swimming forward, reaching out with one hand. I am wearing a sleek, iridescent wetsuit that shimmers like the surface of a bubble, reflecting the starlight. My hair is floating around my face like seaweed. Directly in front of me is a massive Beluga whale, its skin translucent and glowing faintly with internal starlight, looking like a creature made of nebulas. It is facing me, its dark eye gazing into my soul with ancient wisdom. Surrounding us are floating coral reefs made of crystal and star clusters, with jellyfish that look like exploding supernovas drifting by. The lighting is ethereal, coming from the whale and the distant galaxies, casting soft, otherworldly blue and violet glows on my face. The vibe is transcendent, silent, and overwhelmingly beautiful—like two small souls meeting in an infinite universe.

images.alt: A contemplative samurai warrior in side profile, head bowed downward in quiet reflection, long black hair tied in a loose topknot with wild strands blowing freely. He wears ornate layered ō-yoroi armor with segmented black and red lacquered plates trimmed in gold, over a deep navy blue kimono printed with delicate pink and gold cherry blossom patterns. He grips a katana with both hands, blade angled downward. Behind him, a massive crimson red sun dominates the background, partially textured with paint splatter and aged patina effects. To the right, a flowering sakura tree with vivid pink and red blossoms. In the far left background, a snow-capped Mount Fuji rendered in soft blue-grey tones. Fallen petals and ink splatter dots float through the air. The overall style is a hybrid of dramatic digital painting and traditional Japanese woodblock aesthetics, with bold painterly brushwork, high contrast, and a warm amber-cream background. Cinematic composition, ultra-detailed, rich textures, moody and epic atmosphere.

A dark, painterly portrait of a pale young woman framed between two towering horned figures whose forms resemble ancient carved idols or post-human funerary guardians. Her expression is distant and introspective, eyes lowered slightly as loose strands of black hair fall across her face. The smooth porcelain quality of her skin contrasts sharply against the rough, stone-like textures of the beings surrounding her. Subtle golden fracture-like lines trace organically along the side of her neck and collarbone, blending into her anatomy like delicate conductive veins or gilded kintsugi beneath the skin.

She wears a richly textured robe patterned with muted gold floral embroidery that catches soft ambient light without overpowering the image. Her dark hair flows in layered, ink-like strands, visually connecting her to the shadowed figures behind her. The horned entities press close on either side in silent, intimate proximity, their faces partially obscured in darkness and rendered with eroded, sculptural textures that blur the line between stone, synthetic material, and living flesh.

Behind them, the background remains intentionally sparse and subdued, with pale flowering branches emerging softly from gray atmospheric haze. The restrained palette of charcoal black, slate gray, ivory, and muted gold creates a solemn, devotional atmosphere. The overall composition evokes themes of ritual intimacy, post-human preservation, symbolic guardianship, and melancholic sacredness, as though the image were a recovered icon from a future civilization.

A painterly romantic-futurist illustration depicting a young woman seated on a stone ledge overlooking a vast valley city at dusk. She wears a flowing cream-colored dress with delicate lace trim and muted green sleeves, her dark hair loosely gathered with pale pink blossoms woven into it. Her posture is soft and inward, head tilted downward with closed eyes and a weary, contemplative expression. One hand rests against the stone beside her, subtly supporting her weight, while warm evening light brushes gently across her face, chest, and fabric folds.

Behind her, a partially obscured post-human figure embraces her from behind with a heavy mechanical arm wrapped around her waist. The figure’s face is mostly hidden against her shoulder, revealing only fragments of exposed biomechanical anatomy around the jaw, neck, and temple. Tarnished gears, cables, pistons, and articulated metal fingers emerge through a dark coat, blending industrial machinery with mournful elegance. The embrace feels protective yet solemn, as though tenderness survives within something no longer fully human.

The surrounding environment remains calm and painterly: flowering branches frame the upper edges of the composition while distant mountains and a sprawling city dissolve into soft atmospheric haze beneath a muted golden-gray sky. The restrained detail, subdued palette, and emotional stillness create a haunting sense of intimacy, exhaustion, and quiet companionship in a world shaped by post-human transformation.

A richly atmospheric romantic-futurist painting set on a ruined balcony overlooking a vast river city at sunset. At the center sits a pale young woman in a flowing cream-colored dress embroidered with faded floral patterns, softly illuminated by a faint golden halo that separates her from the darkness surrounding her. Her black hair is loosely tied up with stray strands falling around her face, and her expression is distant, melancholic, and quietly introspective as she gazes downward while holding a glass of wine near a small ornate table.

Around her stand three post-human figures rendered in a softer, oil-painted style that makes them feel partially dissolved into shadow and memory. Their forms are humanoid but corrupted by exposed mechanical anatomy, fractured facial structures, rusted halos, and biomechanical decay beneath weathered ceremonial clothing. One leans forward from the left with his face obscured by darkness and tangled metallic textures; another towers behind her like a solemn machine-saint; the third sits to the right with his head bowed in contemplative silence, cables and exposed structures merging into the gloom.

The environment glows with warm amber light from the setting sun, reflecting across rivers and distant domed architecture. Cherry blossoms drift through the air while roses and creeping vines frame the composition. The contrast between the woman’s clarity and the men’s painterly dissolution creates an unsettling emotional tension, suggesting themes of reverence, longing, memory, and the fading boundary between humanity and post-human existence.

Behind her stands a tall post-human figure leaning close, his face partially obscured near her head in an intimate yet unsettling gesture. The remnants of a pale coat hang from his frame like ceremonial ruins. A fractured circular mechanism rises behind him like a rusted halo or ancient machine relic.

The background reveals a sprawling river city glowing beneath an amber sky, blending decayed grandeur, distant towers, and reflective waterways. Cherry blossoms drift through the air while flowers and overgrown foliage frame the lower foreground, contrasting organic fragility against the cold permanence of the post-human figure. The image evokes themes of tenderness, dependency, technological transcendence, and romantic melancholy at the edge of human civilization.

external.description: Many companies are investing heavily in AI but failing to translate isolated productivity gains into meaningful business results. The problem is a “micro-productivity trap,” where firms optimize tasks without rethinking workflows or value creation, preventing organization-wide impact. This article outlines four steps to escape this trap—strategically narrowing use cases, reimagining cross-functional workflows, engaging frontline employees, and measuring outcomes tied to business value—illustrated through examples from companies like Lowe’s and a Fortune 1000 manufacturing firm.
external.title: How to Move from AI Experimentation to AI Transformation

"The CIA Owned Your Secrets for Decades: The Crypto AG Scandal" on @Spreaker #bnd #ciascandal #coldwarespionage #cryptoag #cybersecurity #encryptionbackdoor #geopolitics #hansbuhler #hiddentruths #intelligencecoup #intelligencehistory #modernprivacy #nationalsecurity #sigint

external.description:
🕵️‍♂️ The Ultimate Betrayal: How the CIA Owned Your Secrets for 50 Years

What if every "secure" message sent by world leaders for five decades was actually being BCC’d directly to the CIA? Imagine buying a high-tech vault, only to find out the manufacturer handed the master key to the world’s most powerful spy agencies. This isn't a Hollywood script—this is the chilling reality of Operation Rubicon, arguably the greatest intelligence coup in modern history.

In this episode, we pull back the curtain on the jaw-dropping saga of Crypto AG, the "neutral" Swiss firm that tricked the world. While over 120 countries—including Iran, Libya, and Argentina—trusted their most sensitive state secrets to these encryption devices, they were actually subsidizing their own surveillance. We explore how the CIA and West German BND secretly owned the company, rigging the hardware to ensure no code was unbreakable for Western eyes.

Why this episode is a must-listen:

- The Global Long Con: How Swiss neutrality was used as a front for a massive state-sponsored surveillance operation.
- Historical Puppet Masters: Discover how rigged encryption influenced the outcome of the Falklands War and exposed the truth behind the 1986 Berlin discotheque bombing.
- The Ethical Bloodbath: We tackle the controversial ethical dilemmas of agents who witnessed state-sponsored atrocities in Latin America in real-time but chose silence to protect their "backdoor" access.
- Modern Implications: From Cold War espionage to today's data privacy battles, learn why the Crypto AG scandal is the blueprint for modern digital tracking.
This is a thrilling, controversial look at the geopolitical chess game where nations were pawns and "privacy" was a carefully manufactured illusion. We’re diving into the dark side of intelligence history, where the line between hero and villain isn't just thin—it’s encrypted.

Is any communication truly private? Join us as we deconstruct the massive scale of clandestine missions that reshaped the 20th century.

🚀 Ready to have your mind blown? If you think your data is safe, think again. Subscribe now, leave a 5-star review to help us keep uncovering the truth, and share this episode with anyone who still believes their "encrypted" apps are impenetrable! 🎧✨
external.title: The CIA Owned Your Secrets for Decades: The Crypto AG Scandal

record.text: 📣 New Podcast! "Generational Evolution (1883-2050): The Psychological Shift from Survival to the Age of AI" on @Spreaker #1883to2025 #aiethics #aiexistentialism #anxiousgeneration #brainrot #burnoutculture #digitalatrophy #digitaldetox #epigenetics #fourthturning #futureofhumanity #genealogy

external.description:
Why does modern life feel like a simulation you're failing to win?

Ever wonder why you carry a weight of anxiety that doesn't seem to belong to you? From the spiritual nihilism of the Lost Generation in 1883 to the digital brain rot of Generation Alpha, we are tracing the 150-year evolution of the human psyche. This isn't just history; it’s a forensic investigation into the shadow genealogy of your own mind.

In this episode, we dive deep into:
- The Fourth Turning: Why our current era of instability perfectly mirrors the technological explosion of the late 19th century.
- Genetic Memory & Epigenetics: How the "stiff upper lip" of your Great-Depression-era ancestors is literally hardwired into your nervous system.
- Digital Atrophy: The terrifying reality of how screens are eroding our fundamental social skills and empathy.
- The Meaning Crisis: Why humanity is shifting from physical survival to a state of philosophical irrelevance in the age of Artificial Intelligence.
We explore the burnout generation, the betrayal of the corporate dream, and the looming AI existentialism that threatens to automate our very purpose. Is 2025 just 1883 with better WiFi, or are we the first generation to evolve specifically for the algorithm?

Stop scrolling and start understanding. Whether you're a Millennial facing economic stagnation or a Gen Z survivor of the "phone-free childhood" movement, this episode provides the blueprint for reclaiming your humanity in an automated world.

Subscribe now and join the conversation on how we survive the next stage of human evolution!
external.title: Generational Evolution (1883-2050): The Psychological Shift from Survival to the Age of AI
